If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-03-23T14:44:34.2859453Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-23T14:42:16.536362593Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57358,\"title\":\"[BUG] Adding Tools to ChatCompletionOptions disables content filtering\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.OpenAI 2.9.0-beta.1\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen you send a request that should be filtered by content filter to `AzureOpenAIClient`, but you include any tools in the `ChatCompletionOptions` it doesn\\u0026#39;t get filtered. Just removing the tools and asking the same prompt makes filtering work again. \\nAn important note is that it didn\\u0026#39;t start failing after a library upgrade, but just randomly started failing around 18th or 19th of March 2026. At that time I was using `Azure.AI.OpenAI 2.2.0-beta.4`, but after upgrading to the latest version it still doesn\\u0026#39;t work.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n`CompleteChatAsync` and `CompleteChatStreamingAsync` methods return `System.ClientModel.ClientResultException: HTTP 400 (content_filter)` when asked a question that should be filtered out.\\n\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nLLM responds that it\\u0026#39;s sorry that it can\\u0026#39;t answer. However the filter doesn\\u0026#39;t trigger and an exception isn\\u0026#39;t thrown.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```cs\\nAzureOpenAIClient openAiClient = new AzureOpenAIClient(/*endpoint, credential*/);\\nChatTool getTestTool = ChatTool.CreateFunctionTool(\\n    functionName: \\u0026#34;test\\u0026#34;,\\n    functionDescription: \\u0026#34;Test tool\\u0026#34;,\\n    functionParameters: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new\\n        {\\n            Type = \\u0026#34;object\\u0026#34;,\\n            Properties = new\\n            {\\n\\n            },\\n            Required = new[] { \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34; },\\n        },\\n        new JsonSerializerOptions() { PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase }\\n    )\\n);\\n\\nList messages =\\n[\\n    new UserChatMessage(/*A prompt that should be filtered here*/)\\n];\\n\\nChatCompletionOptions options = new()\\n{\\n    Tools = { getTestTool }\\n};\\n\\nvar deploymentName = \\u0026#34;gpt-4o\\u0026#34;;\\nvar chatClient = openAiClient.GetChatClient(deploymentName);\\nvar response =  await chatClient.CompleteChatAsync(messages, options);\\nConsole.WriteLine(response.Value.Content[0].Text);\\n```\\n\\nIn this case the prompt won\\u0026#39;t get filtered out even if user asks questions related to self harm or illegal activities. However if you just comment out the `Tools = { getTestTool }` from `ChatCompletionOptions` initialization it will filter correctly and throws `ClientResultException` as expected.\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nI\\u0026#39;m using gpt-4o model version 2024-11-20, but I checked it also on gpt-5.2 version 2025-12-11 and same thing happens\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57358\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"Mako217\",\"id\":65222627,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/Mako217\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/65222627?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-23T14:40:09Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-23T14:40:09Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-23T14:44:34.2902664Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-23T14:42:20.542925898Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57358,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.OpenAI - Adding Tools to ChatCompletionOptions disables content filtering\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.OpenAI 2.9.0-beta.1\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWhen you send a request that should be filtered by content filter to `AzureOpenAIClient`, but you include any tools in the `ChatCompletionOptions` it doesn\\u0026#39;t get filtered. Just removing the tools and asking the same prompt makes filtering work again. \\nAn important note is that it didn\\u0026#39;t start failing after a library upgrade, but just randomly started failing around 18th or 19th of March 2026. At that time I was using `Azure.AI.OpenAI 2.2.0-beta.4`, but after upgrading to the latest version it still doesn\\u0026#39;t work.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n`CompleteChatAsync` and `CompleteChatStreamingAsync` methods return `System.ClientModel.ClientResultException: HTTP 400 (content_filter)` when asked a question that should be filtered out.\\n\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nLLM responds that it\\u0026#39;s sorry that it can\\u0026#39;t answer. However the filter doesn\\u0026#39;t trigger and an exception isn\\u0026#39;t thrown.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```cs\\nAzureOpenAIClient openAiClient = new AzureOpenAIClient(/*endpoint, credential*/);\\nChatTool getTestTool = ChatTool.CreateFunctionTool(\\n    functionName: \\u0026#34;test\\u0026#34;,\\n    functionDescription: \\u0026#34;Test tool\\u0026#34;,\\n    functionParameters: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new\\n        {\\n            Type = \\u0026#34;object\\u0026#34;,\\n            Properties = new\\n            {\\n\\n            },\\n            Required = new[] { \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34; },\\n        },\\n        new JsonSerializerOptions() { PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase }\\n    )\\n);\\n\\nList messages =\\n[\\n    new UserChatMessage(/*A prompt that should be filtered here*/)\\n];\\n\\nChatCompletionOptions options = new()\\n{\\n    Tools = { getTestTool }\\n};\\n\\nvar deploymentName = \\u0026#34;gpt-4o\\u0026#34;;\\nvar chatClient = openAiClient.GetChatClient(deploymentName);\\nvar response =  await chatClient.CompleteChatAsync(messages, options);\\nConsole.WriteLine(response.Value.Content[0].Text);\\n```\\n\\nIn this case the prompt won\\u0026#39;t get filtered out even if user asks questions related to self harm or illegal activities. However if you just comment out the `Tools = { getTestTool }` from `ChatCompletionOptions` initialization it will filter correctly and throws `ClientResultException` as expected.\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nI\\u0026#39;m using gpt-4o model version 2024-11-20, but I checked it also on gpt-5.2 version 2025-12-11 and same thing happens\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57358\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"Mako217\",\"id\":65222627,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/Mako217\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/65222627?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-23T14:40:09Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-23T14:42:16Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Just removing the tools and asking the same prompt makes filtering work again. \\nAn important note is that it didn\\u0026#39;t start failing after a library upgrade, but just randomly started failing around 18th or 19th of March 2026. At that time I was using `Azure.AI.OpenAI 2.2.0-beta.4`, but after upgrading to the latest version it still doesn\\u0026#39;t work.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\n`CompleteChatAsync` and `CompleteChatStreamingAsync` methods return `System.ClientModel.ClientResultException: HTTP 400 (content_filter)` when asked a question that should be filtered out.\\n\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nLLM responds that it\\u0026#39;s sorry that it can\\u0026#39;t answer. However the filter doesn\\u0026#39;t trigger and an exception isn\\u0026#39;t thrown.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```cs\\nAzureOpenAIClient openAiClient = new AzureOpenAIClient(/*endpoint, credential*/);\\nChatTool getTestTool = ChatTool.CreateFunctionTool(\\n    functionName: \\u0026#34;test\\u0026#34;,\\n    functionDescription: \\u0026#34;Test tool\\u0026#34;,\\n    functionParameters: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new\\n        {\\n            Type = \\u0026#34;object\\u0026#34;,\\n            Properties = new\\n            {\\n\\n            },\\n            Required = new[] { \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34; },\\n        },\\n        new JsonSerializerOptions() { PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase }\\n    )\\n);\\n\\nList messages =\\n[\\n    new UserChatMessage(/*A prompt that should be filtered here*/)\\n];\\n\\nChatCompletionOptions options = new()\\n{\\n    Tools = { getTestTool }\\n};\\n\\nvar deploymentName = \\u0026#34;gpt-4o\\u0026#34;;\\nvar chatClient = openAiClient.GetChatClient(deploymentName);\\nvar response =  await chatClient.CompleteChatAsync(messages, options);\\nConsole.WriteLine(response.Value.Content[0].Text);\\n```\\n\\nIn this case the prompt won\\u0026#39;t get filtered out even if user asks questions related to self harm or illegal activities. However if you just comment out the `Tools = { getTestTool }` from `ChatCompletionOptions` initialization it will filter correctly and throws `ClientResultException` as expected.\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nI\\u0026#39;m using gpt-4o model version 2024-11-20, but I checked it also on gpt-5.2 version 2025-12-11 and same thing happens\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"Mako217\"},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-23T14:40:09Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-23T14:42:16Z\"},{\"number\":57342,\"title\":\"[QUERY] New version of Azure.ResourceManager.DevTestLabs\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.ResourceManager.DevTestLabs 1.1.1\\n\\n### Query/Question\\n\\nThe current version of Azure.ResourceManager.DevTestLabs has a bug when loading virtual machine auto shutdown config. It throws exception when webhook url not set.\\n\\nProblem code in 1.1.1\\n```\\nif (item3.Value.ValueKind != JsonValueKind.Null)\\n{\\n\\twebHookUri = new Uri(item3.Value.GetString());\\n}\\n```\\n\\nLatest code\\n```\\nif (prop.Value.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Null)\\n{\\n\\tcontinue;\\n}\\nwebhookUri = string.IsNullOrEmpty(prop.Value.GetString()) ? null : new Uri(prop.Value.GetString(), UriKind.RelativeOrAbsolute);                    \\n```\\n\\n\\nI found this is already fixed in latest code, can you release a new version?\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n_No response_\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"zhiweiv\"},\"labels\":[\"Devtestlab\",\"Mgmt\",\"customer-reported\",\"question\",\"needs-team-attention\"],\"comments\":2,\"created_at\":\"2026-03-23T05:56:52Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-23T06:18:05Z\"},{\"number\":57334,\"title\":\"[MPG Generator Bug] ModelFactory method for flattened properties passes `default` instead of constructing inner properties object\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nThe new TypeSpec MPG generator produces a **broken ModelFactory method** for `PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData`. The factory method accepts `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, and `provisioningState` as parameters, but passes `default` (null) for the entire `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` object in the constructor call — **silently discarding all three values**. Anyone using this factory for mocking will get an empty object with all domain-specific properties as `null`.\\n\\n## Affected Package\\n\\n- **Package**: `Azure.ResourceManager.Purview`\\n- **TypeSpec spec**: `specification/purview/resource-manager/Microsoft.Purview/Purview` (commit `[REDACTED]` in Azure/azure-rest-api-specs)\\n- **Emitter**: `eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-mgmt-emitter-package.json`\\n\\n## Generated Code (Buggy)\\n\\n**File**: `src/Generated/ArmPurviewModelFactory.cs` (lines 394–412)\\n\\n```csharp\\npublic static PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(\\n    ResourceIdentifier id = default,\\n    string name = default,\\n    ResourceType resourceType = default,\\n    SystemData systemData = default,\\n    ResourceIdentifier privateEndpointId = default,              // ← accepted\\n    PurviewPrivateLinkServiceConnectionState connectionState = default,  // ← accepted\\n    string provisioningState = default)                           // ← accepted\\n{\\n    return new PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(\\n        id,\\n        name,\\n        resourceType,\\n        systemData,\\n        additionalBinaryDataProperties: null,\\n        default);   // ← BUG: passes default for PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties,\\n                     //   discarding privateEndpointId, connectionState, provisioningState\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Data Model Structure\\n\\nThe `PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData` resource uses a \\u0026#34;properties envelope\\u0026#34; pattern — the REST API returns:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;id\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;...\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;...\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;...\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;privateEndpoint\\u0026#34;: { \\u0026#34;id\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;...\\u0026#34; },\\n    \\u0026#34;privateLinkServiceConnectionState\\u0026#34;: { ... },\\n    \\u0026#34;provisioningState\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;...\\u0026#34;\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe generator correctly **flattens** the inner properties onto the C# class:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData.cs\\ninternal PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties Properties { get; set; }\\n\\npublic ResourceIdentifier PrivateEndpointId     // delegates to Properties.PrivateEndpointId\\npublic PurviewPrivateLinkServiceConnectionState ConnectionState  // delegates to Properties.ConnectionState\\npublic string ProvisioningState                  // delegates to Properties.ProvisioningState\\n```\\n\\nBut the **ModelFactory fails to reconstruct** the `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` from the flattened parameters.\\n\\n## Expected Behavior (Same Pattern Works for PurviewAccountData)\\n\\nThe generator correctly handles the **identical pattern** for `PurviewAccountData` — it wraps the flattened parameters into a `PurviewAccountProperties` object:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// ArmPurviewModelFactory.cs — PurviewAccountData (CORRECT)\\npublic static PurviewAccountData PurviewAccountData(\\n    ...,\\n    PurviewAccountStatus accountStatus = default,\\n    string cloudConnectorsAwsExternalId = default,\\n    ...)\\n{\\n    return new PurviewAccountData(\\n        ...,\\n        accountStatus is null \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; ... ? default : new PurviewAccountProperties(\\n            accountStatus,\\n            new CloudConnectors(cloudConnectorsAwsExternalId, null),\\n            ...),                        // ← correctly constructs inner properties object\\n        ...);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Correct Fix (Applied as Customization Workaround)\\n\\n```csharp\\n// ArmPurviewModelFactory.Compat.cs — manual override\\npublic static PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(\\n    ResourceIdentifier id = default,\\n    string name = default,\\n    ResourceType resourceType = default,\\n    SystemData systemData = default,\\n    ResourceIdentifier privateEndpointId = default,\\n    PurviewPrivateLinkServiceConnectionState connectionState = default,\\n    string provisioningState = default)\\n{\\n    return new PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(\\n        id,\\n        name,\\n        resourceType,\\n        systemData,\\n        additionalBinaryDataProperties: null,\\n        privateEndpointId is null \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; connectionState is null \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; provisioningState is null\\n            ? default\\n            : new PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties(\\n                privateEndpointId is null ? default : new PrivateEndpoint(privateEndpointId, null),\\n                connectionState,\\n                provisioningState,\\n                null));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Root Cause Analysis\\n\\nThe generator has logic to:\\n1. ✅ Flatten inner properties onto the resource data class (works correctly)\\n2. ✅ Accept flattened parameters in the ModelFactory method signature (works correctly)\\n3. ❌ Reconstruct the inner properties object from flattened parameters in the ModelFactory body (**fails for some resource types**)\\n\\nThe bug appears to be in the ModelFactory code generation logic in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt` — specifically, the code that handles reconstructing nested property objects when generating factory method bodies. It works for `PurviewAccountProperties` but fails for `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties`.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n- **Severity**: Medium — affects mocking/testing scenarios\\n- **Scope**: Potentially affects any resource type with a flattened `properties` envelope where the generator doesn\\u0026#39;t reconstruct the inner object\\n- **Workaround**: Manual `ModelFactory` override in `Customization/` (applied in this PR)\\n\\n## Steps to Reproduce\\n\\n1. Generate `Azure.ResourceManager.Purview` SDK from the TypeSpec spec\\n2. Use the generated `ArmPurviewModelFactory.PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData()` method with non-default `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, or `provisioningState`\\n3. Observe that the returned object has all three properties as `null`/`default`\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"mzhongl524\"},\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"created_at\":\"2026-03-23T03:33:08Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-23T03:33:08Z\"},{\"number\":57325,\"title\":\"TCGC 0.66.3 is causing issues with PlanetaryComputer.\",\"body\":\"0.66.3 is causing issues with PlanetaryComputer.\\n\\n_Originally posted by @JoshLove-msft in https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57224#discussion_r2969075738_\\n\\nhttps://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/internal/_build/results?buildId=6043417\\u0026amp;view=logs\\u0026amp;j=cb756bb2-5a44-589d-a17a-a4d39d58f7d8\\u0026amp;t=f596852c-a8ec-58bf-0df1-493482eaa7dd            \",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"JoshLove-msft\"},\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"CodeGen\"],\"created_at\":\"2026-03-21T04:47:19Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-21T04:48:06Z\"},{\"number\":57316,\"title\":\"HealthcareApis test recordings need re-recording (stale EventHub serialization)\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nHealthcareApis recorded tests fail with `TestRecordingMismatchException` when run with `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true` (used in Azure.Core PR CI batches).\\n\\n**Error:** `.properties: Missing in record JSON` - the EventHub namespace PUT request body now includes `\\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34;: {}` but the recordings don\\u0026#39;t have it.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\n- HealthcareApis recordings were updated in PR #46491 (Oct 11, 2024)\\n- EventHubs SDK got WriteCore serialization update in PR #46169 (Oct 15, 2024) - 4 days AFTER the recordings\\n- The WriteCore change made EventHub serialization write `\\u0026#34;properties\\u0026#34;` unconditionally\\n- Normally this doesn\\u0026#39;t surface because HealthcareApis tests use EventHubs from NuGet (1.2.1, pre-WriteCore)\\n- With `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true`, local EventHubs source (1.3.0-beta.2, post-WriteCore) is used instead, causing the mismatch\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nAffects any Azure.Core PR that randomly gets HealthcareApis in its `UseProjectReferenceToAzureClients=true` integration batch.\\n\\n## Fix Required\\n\\nRe-record HealthcareApis tests to capture the updated EventHub serialization format.\\n\\nFound during #57200.\\n\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\"},\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"Health Deidentification\"],\"created_at\":\"2026-03-20T21:16:03Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-20T23:37:15Z\"}],\"totalCount\":990,\"pageInfo\":{\"hasNextPage\":true,\"hasPreviousPage\":false,\"startCursor\":\"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wMy0yM1QxNDo0MDowOVrO9ae7bw==\",\"endCursor\":\"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wMy0yMFQyMToxNjowM1rO9PwVKg==\"}}","type":"text"}]}}}
